---
title: "Identity Modernization Spec"
subtitle: "Identity plane governance and migration specification"
doc-type: modernization-spec
audience: [Customer, Architect, Assessor]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md"
  - "docs/customer-documents/architecture-series/aodim-architecture.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/whitepapers/modernization-governance-whitepaper.qmd"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# Identity Modernization Spec

## Scope

Cross-adapter specification for identity modernization under the UIAO
substrate. Covers the directory transition (typically AD → Entra ID),
attribute model, group computation, delegation, and the canonical claim
emissions identity adapters must produce. Vendor-specific detail lives
in adapter specs (see [Adapter Specs](../../adapter-specs/index.html));
this spec is the substrate contract every identity adapter inherits.

[IMAGE-01: Identity modernization domain diagram. Center shows the canonical Identity Plane as a labeled rectangle with sub-zones for Attributes, Computed Groups, Delegation, and Authentication context. Around the perimeter, four modernization adapter slots labeled (entra-id, active-directory, piv-usaccess, attribute-services) connect with bidirectional arrows to the central plane. Below the plane, a thin horizontal bar labeled "AODIM canonical attribute set: assignment scope, organizational role, classification clearance" anchors the model. Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.]

## Substrate contract

Every identity adapter inherits four properties:

- **`mission-class: identity`** — declared in
  `adapter-registry.yaml` or `modernization-registry.yaml`.
- **`object-identity-only: true`** — claims resolve to identity-plane
  objects, not opaque strings.
- **`certificate-anchored: true`** — mTLS-anchored emission of
  canonical claims.
- **`gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`** (or named exception) — operates
  within the authorization boundary.

These are schema-validated on every PR; an identity adapter that
violates any of them does not merge.

## Adapters in scope (Phase 1+)

| Adapter | Class | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `active-directory` | modernization | Source-state observer for AD-to-Entra migration |
| `entra-id` | modernization | Target-state authority post-migration |
| `entra-policy-targeting` | modernization | Conditional access, PIM, entitlement management mapping |
| `piv-usaccess` | conformance | PIV credentialing surface (AAL/IAL evidence) |
| `va-veteran-attribute-service` | conformance | Authoritative VA attribute source |
| `uscis-immigration-attribute-service` | conformance | Authoritative USCIS attribute source |
| `treasury-ofac-attribute-service` | conformance | Authoritative OFAC attribute source |

The list grows as additional identity adapters register.

## Integration shape: AODIM

Per the [AODIM Architecture](../../architecture-series/aodim-architecture.qmd),
identities carry **attributes**, and access is **computed** from those
attributes. Identity adapters emit canonical claims in this shape:

```yaml
provenance:
  claim_id: "urn:uiao:claim:identity:{attribute_kind}:{uuid}"
  issuer_identity: "{entra_object_id | agency_issuer_id}"
  source_classification: "authoritative | derived | synthesized"
  ...
attributes:
  assignment_scope: "{scope}"
  organizational_role: "{role}"
  classification_clearance: "{clearance_level}"
  ...
```

The three attributes named above are the canonical set; downstream
policies consume them. Additional attributes are allowed but must be
declared in canon.

## Control mapping

The identity domain maps onto NIST 800-53 IA / AC families and onto
CISA ZTMM Identity pillar. KSI rules under
`src/uiao/rules/ksi/iam/` declare the bindings. Each adapter
declaration in `modernization-registry.yaml` references the KSI rules
it satisfies.

## Drift considerations

Identity domain drift surfaces as:

- **`DRIFT-IDENTITY`** — issuer cannot be resolved; `P1` severity,
  halt + alert.
- **`DRIFT-AUTHZ`** — identity claim emitted outside its consent
  envelope; `P1`/`P2` depending on liveness.
- **`DRIFT-SCHEMA`** — attribute structure mismatch; `P2` severity.
- **`DRIFT-PROVENANCE`** — chain of custody broken across the
  AD → Entra cutover; `P2`/`P3` depending on liveness.

Cutover periods elevate drift sensitivity; the substrate's tempo
applies regardless.

## Honest limits

- Runtime detection of `DRIFT-IDENTITY` and `DRIFT-AUTHZ` is partial
  to design-only across the adapter fleet. Coverage is growing.
- Migration-time evidence (claims emitted during the AD → Entra
  cutover window) requires both source and target adapters running;
  partial deployment loses the bridge evidence.
- The AODIM attribute set is opinionated. Programs that elect to keep
  an OU-driven Entra deployment will not realize most of the
  spec's leverage.

## Validation pairing

This spec pairs with the
[Identity Validation Suite](../../validation-suites/domains/identity/identity.qmd)
under Phase 6. Together they form the identity-domain MTS/MVS pair.

## Related documents

- [Modernization Specs Index](../index.qmd)
- [Architecture Series — AODIM](../../architecture-series/aodim-architecture.qmd)
- [Whitepaper: Modernization Governance](../../whitepapers/modernization-governance-whitepaper.qmd)
- [Modernization Registry (canon)](../../../../src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml)
